meaning: adj.嗤之以鼻的,自命不凡的
examples: Western firms such as IBM and Accenture can be sniffy about their Indian rivals' sophistication.
He is always puffing about the expensive wines he brings over to my place, and is then sniffy about the offerings I take round to his.
In an industry in which visionaries are often sniffy about anyone else's ideas, the readiness to go elsewhere proved a devastating advantage.
British academics have become less sniffy about commerce now that more of their colleagues are buying fancy cars.
Apparently, quite a few of his best students drop computer science at A-level because top universities are still sniffy about it.
